summary: Phase 11.2 — Game Canvas Repaints & Gold-Luxe Polish. PURE FRONTEND visual repaint validation across 9 pages (/, /wheel, /crash, /plinko, /mines, /roulette, /battles, /achievements, /inventory) at 360x800. ALL 9 pages: zero horizontal overflow (scrollWidth === clientWidth === 360), zero JS page errors, zero React error overlays, load times 1.9–2.7s. Only console noise is the benign Telegram WebApp polyfill warning ('Method requestFullscreen is not supported in version 6.0'). Visual screenshots confirm gold-luxe chrome on Mines (gold border, gold 'Start round' btn, rose slider preserved), Plinko (gold hero pill, gold row/risk active state, gold 'Drop ball' btn), Battles (gold 'Create' CTA, gold 'OPEN' status pill, gold entry TON, gold 'Join' btn, HIGH WINS/LOW WINS emerald/rose semantic preserved), Inventory (gold 'Withdrawals', 'Withdraw' panel, 'Deposit Telegram Gift' panel, 'OWNED' tab gold-bright). Strict forbidden-palette scan (`fuchsia-`, `violet-`, `purple-`, `pink-`, `amber-`, `orange-` raw tailwind tokens — these are NOT remapped in tailwind.config.js, unlike `cyan-*`/`teal-*`/`sky-*`/`cyber-*` which are all aliased to the gold palette) returned ONLY intentional semantic/global hits across all 8 game pages: (a) season-hud Battle-Pass border/flame icon and vip-badge Bronze tier are warm amber-300/amber-700 — that IS the gold-luxe family, by design; (b) Crash history pills use bg-amber-500/15 for multipliers in 1.5×–5× tier band — explicitly called out in the problem statement as INTENTIONAL semantic tier-band colour; (c) the dev-credit fab '+100 TON' button uses orange-500 — dev-only affordance, fixed-positioned, never in production user view; (d) Roulette 'Epic' rarity GiftCard uses purple-500 — industry-standard loot-rarity semantic (gray→blue→purple→orange), arguably semantic and not decorative chrome; flagging as a soft observation only. ZERO regressions. Phase 11.2 visual goals MET.

issues: GiftCard 'Epic' rarity badge on data-testid='roulette-gift-daily_jackpot' uses bg-purple-500/20 + border-purple-500/45 + text-purple-200 — Tailwind purple-* is NOT remapped to gold (unlike cyan/teal/sky/cyber-*). This is industry-standard rarity semantic (Epic = purple) so it is likely INTENTIONAL, but if the brief expects 100% gold chrome on rarity badges too, update common/GiftCard.jsx rarity-styles map. Verified ONLY rarity badge — not the card chrome, which is gold. Low priority / probably intentional.

action_items: (P3 / optional) Confirm whether 'Epic' rarity GiftCard badge should remain purple (industry-standard rarity semantic) or be flipped to gold like other chrome. Affects common/GiftCard.jsx rarity styles. No other regressions found.
critical_code_review_comments: tailwind.config.js (lines 49–78) cleanly remaps cyber-cyan/cyber-purple/cyber-magenta/cyan-*/teal-*/sky-* palettes to the warm gold scale — a clever zero-rename strategy that keeps 100+ legacy class names while delivering the new visual identity. boxShadow neon-cyan/neon-purple/neon-magenta (lines 124–127) also remapped to gold glows. Solid systemic approach.
All 9 pages at 360x800 report scrollWidth === clientWidth === 360 — Phase 10 overflow audit baseline preserved across the repaint. No new layout regressions.
Crash history pills correctly use the rose<1.5×/amber<5×/emerald<25×/yellow>=25× tier-band as game-information signal per spec — not decorative chrome.
Mines hero: rose-tinted bomb icon + rose Mines-count slider preserved as danger-semantic per spec; the surrounding card chrome (border, 'Start round' CTA) is gold-luxe. Correct semantic separation.
Battles list status 'OPEN' pill is gold, but 'HIGH WINS'/'LOW WINS' mode pill remains emerald/rose — semantic separation correctly observed.
Telegram WebApp 'requestFullscreen not supported in version 6.0' console error is library-level polyfill noise, not a regression. Same noise present in iteration_10 baseline.

dev-credit-fab '+100 TON' button uses orange-500 gradient — acceptable for dev-only staging affordance, but for production-readiness consider migrating to gold for consistency (currently masked by `?dev=1` gate).

success_rate.frontend: 9/9 pages = 100% (no overflow, no JS errors, all chrome repaints verified)

test_credentials: Dev bypass via ?dev=1 (ENABLE_DEV_LOGIN=true) — no JWT needed. Auto-created sandbox user (e.g. @dev_960...). Admin tg=100000001 unchanged.
seed_data_creation: None — pure visual repaint validation, no data writes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 11.2 is visually clean. When auditing future phases, remember tailwind.config.js (lines 45–78) remaps `cyber-cyan`/`cyber-purple`/`cyber-magenta` AND raw `cyan-*`/`teal-*`/`sky-*` palettes to the warm gold scale — so classname-based color scans must EXCLUDE those keywords and look only at `fuchsia-`/`violet-`/`purple-`/`pink-`/`amber-`/`orange-` raw tokens to find real regressions. Semantic-safe palettes per spec: rose-* (danger), emerald-* (win), red/black/green roulette tiles, and Crash multiplier rose<1.5×/amber<5×/emerald<25×/yellow>=25× tier bands. The dev-credit-fab `+100 TON` button (orange-500) is dev-only chrome and should be ignored. Industry-standard loot rarity colors (gray/blue/purple/orange) on GiftCard rarity badges are arguably semantic; verify intent before flagging. Screenshots saved at /app/.screenshots/phase11_2_*.png for nine pages.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs reproduced. Phase 11.2 visual goals all met.
